build: Build #33 FINAL — 200 OK + SSE with 9 plain text chunks + done event (exact real Gamma format from HAR #029)
console_errors: [PromptAndOutlineSettings] error generating outline: Unable to complete request

diagnosis.verdict: BLOCKED_ONLY — Warning delivery via SSE response injection is NOT feasible for Gamma
diagnosis.response_delivery: CONSISTENTLY FAILED — ERR_CONNECTION_CLOSED on every build since #30

builds_tested_summary.build_21_422: PARTIAL_PASS — error toast, no warning text

key_findings: 1. Only Build #26 ever delivered data to Gamma's UI (vertical chars, not readable warning)
2. Builds #30-#33 all get ERR_CONNECTION_CLOSED despite 200 OK headers — HTTP/2 stream closes before body delivery
3. Content format (JSON, plain text, multi-chunk) does NOT matter — the failure is at HTTP/2 transport layer
4. Body size does NOT matter (tested small to ~2.5kB)
5. Even exact real Gamma SSE format fails — proving the issue is APF's HTTP/2 frame delivery, not content
6. Build #26's success was likely due to specific use_end_stream=false + use_goaway=false flags that are no longer being applied correctly in newer builds
diagnosis.recommendation: For Gamma: use BLOCK_ONLY strategy (HTTP error codes like 403/422 which reliably trigger error toasts). Warning text injection via SSE is not achievable with current APF HTTP/2 implementation. If warning text is required, consider: (1) Fix HTTP/2 DATA frame delivery to match Build #26 exactly, (2) Use a completely different approach like DNS-level redirect to a warning page.
diagnosis.conclusion: FINAL VERDICT: Gamma warning delivery via SSE response injection is NOT feasible. Recommend BLOCK_ONLY strategy.
